overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto has a significant social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, totaling 3580 followers. His content primarily focuses on research, tools, and job opportunities related to computational social science and disinformation studies. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn showing the highest individual post engagement.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research papers, grant announcements, and discussions related to computational methods for social media analysis.
Focus on coordinated inauthentic behavior, information operations, and methodological approaches to studying disinformation.
Announcing job openings (postdoctoral researchers) and research opportunities (PhD students) in the field.
Sharing new tool releases related to detecting coordinated link sharing behavior.
Promoting conference presentations related to computational methods for detecting coordinated campaigns

recommendations: Diversify content formats to include videos, infographics, and live sessions to increase engagement.
Tailor content to each platform's audience and best practices (e.g., more professional tone on LinkedIn).
Engage more actively with followers by responding to comments and participating in relevant conversations.
Use relevant hashtags to increase content discoverability and reach a wider audience.
Cross-promote content across platforms to maximize visibility.
Analyze post performance regularly to identify trends and optimize content strategy.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows moderate engagement with a strong focus on research publications and opportunities. Reposts are a significant engagement metric on this platform.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has the lowest follower count and engagement, but focuses on sharing research tools and participating in broader academic discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the highest follower count and engagement, with posts related to grant announcements and job opportunities performing particularly well. Comments are a unique engagement metric on this platform.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
